low_confidence.brief_title: Study Evaluating The Coadministration of Begacestat And Donepezil
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Begacestat is a BACE inhibitor (amyloid production mechanism), but trial focuses on drug-drug interaction safety in healthy subjects, not AD efficacy. [candidate_entity: begacestat]

low_confidence.brief_title: A Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Dose Ranging Study of Xanamem® in Healthy Elderly Volunteers
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Xanamem mechanism not specified in abstract; insufficient detail to assign to specific bucket. [candidate_entity: Xanamem]

low_confidence.brief_title: Effects of Sage on Memory and Mental Performance in Alzheimer's Disease Patients
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Sage (Salvia officinalis) is a cognitive enhancer with potential cholinergic activity, but mechanism remains unclear and poorly characterized in AD research.

low_confidence.brief_title: Efficacy Study of a ZT-1 Implant in Patients Suffering From Alzheimer's Disease
low_confidence.classification_rationale: ZT-1 mechanism unclear; trial compares implant to donepezil but ZT-1's target mechanism is not disclosed in summary.

low_confidence.brief_title: DUVAX: A Phase 1 Alzheimer's Vaccine Study Targeting Amyloid-Beta and Tau
low_confidence.classification_rationale: DUVAX is a dual-target vaccine against both amyloid-beta and tau; no single bucket accommodates combinatorial immunotherapy targeting both pathologies simultaneously. [candidate_entity: DUVAX]

low_confidence.brief_title: A Phase 2a Study to Evaluate the Effect of Rilapladib (SB-659032) in Alzheimer's Disease
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Rilapladib is a lipoprotein-associated phospholipase A2 inhibitor; mechanism does not match any defined taxonomy bucket. [candidate_entity: rilapladib (SB-659032)]

low_confidence.brief_title: CPC-201 Alzheimer's Disease Type Dementia: PET Study
low_confidence.classification_rationale: CPC-201 mechanism unclear; trial primary outcome is donepezil MTD, a symptomatic cholinesterase inhibitor, but CPC-201's own target undefined.

low_confidence.brief_title: Treatment of Apathy in Alzheimer's Disease With Modafinil
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Modafinil is a symptomatic agent affecting wakefulness and neurotransmission, but apathy treatment is peripheral to core AD mechanisms.

low_confidence.brief_title: Agitation in Alzheimer's Disease
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Trazodone and haloperidol are symptomatic agents affecting neurotransmission, though neither is disease-modifying.

low_confidence.brief_title: A Study Evaluating The Absorption Of Dimebon Into The Body From A Dimebon Solution Applied To The Skin
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Dimebon (latrepirdine) has multi-target neuroprotective properties including mitochondrial and synaptic effects, best fitting symptomatic/synaptic category despite broader mechanism.

low_confidence.brief_title: SSRI's and the Rate of Progression From MCI to Dementia
low_confidence.classification_rationale: SSRIs modulate neurotransmission; mechanism fits symptomatic/neurotransmitter category, though indirect compared to primary AD pathways.

low_confidence.brief_title: Progression Delaying Effect of Escitalopram in Alzheimer's Disease
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Escitalopram is an SSRI affecting serotonin neurotransmission; symptomatically relevant but not disease-modifying.

low_confidence.brief_title: Nefiracetam in the Treatment of Alzheimer's Disease
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Nefiracetam is a nootropic agent with symptomatic effects on cognition via membrane fluidity and neurotransmitter modulation, fitting synaptic mechanisms.

low_confidence.brief_title: Immunohistochemical Study of Neurodegenerative Diseases
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Donepezil is the primary drug intervention; however, study is primarily an immunohistochemical mechanistic investigation rather than a donepezil efficacy trial.

low_confidence.brief_title: Magnesium L-Threonate for the Enhancement of Learning and Memory in People With Dementia
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Magnesium L-Threonate targets synaptic plasticity and NMDA receptor function; fits synaptic mechanism though not core representative agent.

low_confidence.brief_title: A Phase 1 Study To Evaluate The Pharmacokinetics, Safety, And Tolerability Of Dimebon [PF-01913539] In Japanese And Western Healthy Subjects
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Dimebon is a mitochondrial function modulator with putative neuroprotective and synaptic effects; mechanism not precisely fitting any bucket.

low_confidence.brief_title: Endothelial Facilitation in Alzheimer's Disease
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Simvastatin is a statin targeting cholesterol/lipid metabolism; L-Arginine and sapropterin target endothelial function and blood flow rather than core AD pathology mechanisms.

low_confidence.brief_title: A 12-week Extension Trial to Evaluate the Safety and Tolerability of Brexpiprazole in the Treatment of Subjects With Agitation Associated With Dementia of the Alzheimer's Type
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Brexpiprazole is an atypical antipsychotic affecting dopamine/serotonin signaling; symptomatic agent for agitation management, not disease-modifying.

low_confidence.brief_title: Study of ITI-007 in Healthy Geriatric Volunteers and in Geriatric Patients With Dementia
low_confidence.classification_rationale: ITI-007 is a serotonin-dopamine modulator affecting synaptic neurotransmission, matching symptomatic/synaptic agents, though primary outcome is safety rather than mechanism-focused efficacy.

low_confidence.brief_title: Escitalopram for Agitation in Alzheimer's Disease
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Escitalopram is an SSRI affecting serotonergic neurotransmission, closest to synaptic/neurotransmitter category despite being symptomatic rather than mechanism-targeted.

low_confidence.brief_title: Methylphenidate for Apathy in Alzheimer's Dementia: A Controlled Study
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Methylphenidate is a sympathomimetic affecting dopamine/norepinephrine neurotransmission for symptomatic apathy management.

low_confidence.brief_title: A Pilot of Methylphenidate in Mild Cognitive Impairment and Dementia Participants.
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Methylphenidate is a sympathomimetic affecting dopamine/norepinephrine neurotransmission, aligning with synaptic mechanisms, though primarily symptomatic rather than disease-modifying.

low_confidence.brief_title: Escitalopram Treatment of Patients With Agitated Dementia
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Escitalopram is an SSRI affecting serotonin neurotransmission; primarily symptomatic for behavioral management rather than disease-modifying.

low_confidence.brief_title: Apoe Impact Study on Brain Structure and Function, in a Population 45 to 75 Years Old
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Study focused on APOE impact on brain structure; mechanism appears APOE-related but intervention not explicitly stated.

low_confidence.brief_title: Safety and Tolerability of Dimebon in Patients on Memantine, and Memantine Plus Donepezil
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Dimebon is a symptomatic agent affecting mitochondrial function and synaptic transmission; trial evaluates safety with memantine and donepezil.

low_confidence.brief_title: A Phase 3 Study Evaluating Safety and Effectiveness of Immune Globulin Intravenous (IGIV 10%) for the Treatment of Mild-to-Moderate Alzheimer´s Disease
low_confidence.classification_rationale: IGIV is a broad immunomodulatory agent; likely modulates complement and innate immunity, though mechanism in AD is not explicitly detailed.

low_confidence.brief_title: An Extension To The B1451006 Protocol To Evaluate The Safety and Efficacy of Dimebon In Subjects With Moderate-to-Severe Alzheimer's Disease
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Dimebon (latrepirdine) is a symptomatic agent with mitochondrial and neuroprotective properties, closest to synaptic support mechanisms.

low_confidence.brief_title: Serotonergic Pharmacotherapy for Agitation of Dementia
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Citalopram is a serotonergic agent affecting neurotransmission, though trial focuses on symptom management rather than disease mechanism.

low_confidence.brief_title: To Evaluate Effects of AZD1446, Placebo and Donepezil in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Donepezil comparator suggests synaptic mechanism, but AZD1446's specific target mechanism is unclear from abstract.

low_confidence.brief_title: Effects of an Anti-Inflammatory Drug in Alzheimer's Disease
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Cyclophosphamate is an anti-inflammatory agent; mechanism aligns with neuroinflammation modulation, though specificity unclear.

low_confidence.brief_title: Do HMG CoA Reductase Inhibitors Affect Abeta Levels?
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Statins may indirectly reduce amyloid-beta production via lipid/cholesterol modulation, but mechanism is tangential to primary amyloid-focused buckets.

low_confidence.brief_title: Escitalopram for the Treatment of Depression in Alzheimer's Disease
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Escitalopram is an SSRI affecting serotonin neurotransmission; addresses depression symptomatically rather than core AD pathology.

low_confidence.brief_title: Improving Beta-2 Adrenergic Signaling in Alzheimer's Disease
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Formoterol is a beta-2 adrenergic agonist affecting neurotransmission signaling; mechanism doesn't perfectly align with symptomatic agents but closest fit available.

low_confidence.brief_title: Treating Behavioral Disturbances in Individuals With Dementia
low_confidence.classification_rationale: Citalopram (SSRI) and risperidone (antipsychotic) modulate neurotransmission for behavioral symptom management rather than target disease pathology.

watchlist.note: Numerous independent trials (probiotics, synbiotics, fecal transplant) recur but represent a loosely defined 'gut-brain axis' mechanism without a clear unifying molecular target; watching for convergence.

watchlist.entity: oral microbial/infection hypothesis agents (COR388, valacyclovir, doxycycline+rifampin, LHP588, rifaximin)
watchlist.note: Diverse antimicrobial/antiviral agents recur under an infection-hypothesis theme, but mechanisms are heterogeneous (P. gingivalis, HSV, gut microbiota) — needs more consistent clustering before proposing.
Two distinct sponsors/agents recur across many trials; borderline overlap with existing synaptic_neurotransmitter bucket — watching to see if reviewers want it split out.
Single-agent program (Cassava Sciences) across many trials; mechanism unique but no second agent to confirm class-level pattern.

watchlist.entity: GSK3-beta inhibitors / lithium (tideglusib, NP031112, lithium carbonate)
watchlist.note: Recurring across multiple independent trials but mechanism (tau phosphorylation via GSK3) may already be covered under existing tau_targeted bucket's 'phosphorylation' term.
Single-agent program (Anavex) across multiple trials; no second distinct sigma-1 agonist observed yet.
Single-agent program (vTv Therapeutics) across multiple trials; no second distinct RAGE-targeting agent yet observed to confirm a mechanism-level cluster.
Recurs across a few trials but may already be reasonably captured by existing neuroinflammation_microglia_complement bucket; watching for additional distinct TNF-targeted agents.
Only one agent across two trials; insufficient distinct-agent diversity to confirm a mechanism cluster yet.
No second distinct senolytic agent observed yet; monitor for diversification within the proposed senolytic bucket.
